question: What are the join types in PySpark and how does the broadcast join hint work?
answer:
PySpark join types:
  df1.join(df2, on='key', how='inner')   # default
  df1.join(df2, on='key', how='left')
  df1.join(df2, on='key', how='right')
  df1.join(df2, on='key', how='outer')   # full outer
  df1.join(df2, on='key', how='semi')    # left semi: only left rows with a match
  df1.join(df2, on='key', how='anti')    # left anti: only left rows WITHOUT a match
  df1.join(df2, on='key', how='cross')   # cartesian product (no key)

Multiple join keys:
  df1.join(df2, on=['user_id', 'date'], how='inner')

Broadcast join hint:
When one side is small (<= spark.sql.autoBroadcastJoinThreshold, default 10MB), Spark auto-broadcasts. Force it:
  from pyspark.sql.functions import broadcast
  df_large.join(broadcast(df_small), on='key', how='inner')

Why broadcast joins matter:
- Avoids shuffle (most expensive Spark operation)
- Small table sent to ALL executors and stored in memory
- Dramatically faster when one DataFrame fits in executor memory
- AQE can auto-detect and broadcast at runtime if adaptive.enabled=true

Common pitfall: joining on different column types (e.g., string vs int key) silently produces no matches.
